answers.field_label: Time Frame If Print Job Is Ongoing
answers.value: Monthly mailings generated on or about the 5th business day of each month, with potential additional ad-hoc batches (no more than two per month) for catch-up records or urgent corrections. Anticipated start date: 2025-02-01; expected to continue indefinitely, subject to contract renewal and program funding.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: This confidential print and mail job covers individualized Vital Records correspondence that may include partial or full Social Security numbers, dates of birth, and other personally identifiable health information for Indiana residents.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: Typical pieces include: (1) certified birth record correction notices, (2) paternity affidavit confirmation letters, and (3) record amendment approval/denial notices, each printed on secure stock and inserted into double-window security envelopes for USPS First-Class mailing.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: Data files will be transmitted via the State-approved secure file transfer protocol (SFTP) using encrypted CSV or PDF output. All overruns, misprints, and spoiled pieces must be cross-cut shredded on site the same business day after production completion, with no samples retained unless explicitly requested in writing by IDOH program management.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
